audit_metadata.auditor_role: Principal Software Architect — Google Apps Script + Domain-Driven Design
audit_metadata.audit_scope: Architecture-level analysis: coupling, SRP, modules, layering, patterns
audit_metadata.methodology: Static code analysis + pattern matching against 16 Immutable Laws + DDD principles

architecture_tech_debt.title: Group 2 writes directly to FACT_DELIVERY bypassing TransactionService
architecture_tech_debt.description: applyAllPendingDecisions() performs direct setValues() on FACT_DELIVERY sheet instead of delegating to 11_TransactionService.upsertFactDelivery(). This violates Single Writer pattern where TransactionService should be sole owner of FACT_DELIVERY writes.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Refactor to call upsertFactDelivery() for each row in batch. If performance is concern, add batchUpsertFactDelivery() to TransactionService.

architecture_tech_debt.law_violated: Law 1: Single Writer (CRITICAL) + Law 2: SRP (Domain Boundary)
architecture_tech_debt.title: Group 2 creates Master Destination records via gateway wrapper
architecture_tech_debt.description: reprocCreateDestinationViaGateway_() creates Destination master records even though Group 2 should be consumer-only. The code acknowledges this as 'Module Boundary violation' but uses a facade wrapper that hides rather than fixes the violation.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence:
/**
 * reprocCreateDestinationViaGateway_ — [REF-001] Delegate createDestination through Group 1 public helper
 *   แทนการเรียก createDestination() โดยตรงจาก Group 2 (Module Boundary violation)
 */
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Queue master creation requests for async processing by Group 1. ReviewReprocessor should only update Q_REVIEW status, not trigger master writes.
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Domain Boundary Enforcement / Async Command Pattern

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,796 lines with mixed CRUD + UUID resolution + migration + lookup responsibilities
architecture_tech_debt.description: 21_AliasService contains 30+ functions handling Alias CRUD, UUID-to-Person/Place mapping, legacy migration tools, fastLookupByShipToName, alias binding, and confidence scoring. This is 18x the 100-line threshold.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: File has 1,796 lines with public functions: createGlobalAlias(), resolveMasterUuidViaGlobalAlias(), convertUuidToPersonId(), fastLookupByShipToName(), assignMasterUuidIfMissing(), bindAlias(), plus migration functions
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Split into: AliasCrudService (~400 lines), AliasResolver (~300 lines), AliasMigration (~400 lines), AliasLookup (~200 lines)
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Extract Service Pattern / Role Interface Segregation

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,419 lines mixing Person and Place normalization logic
architecture_tech_debt.description: NormalizeService handles Thai prefix stripping for BOTH Person names AND Place addresses, phone extraction, document ID extraction, company suffix handling, phonetic key generation, and semantic note parsing. Violates SRP by combining multiple entity normalizers.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Public functions: runNormalize(), normalizePersonNameFull(), normalizePlaceName(), buildThaiPhoneticKey(), normalizeForCompare(), parseAndStoreSemanticNotes()
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Split into: PersonNormalizer (~500 lines), PlaceNormalizer (~400 lines), SemanticNoteParser (~300 lines)
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Extract Strategy Pattern / Entity Normalizer Factory

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,132 lines with tight coupling to 4+ other modules
architecture_tech_debt.description: PlaceService contains CRUD, multi-strategy search, province extraction, postcode lookup, and geo-enrichment orchestration. Depends on 05_NormalizeService, 16_GeoDictionaryBuilder, 20_ThGeoService, and 21_AliasService simultaneously.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Depends on: 05_NormalizeService, 16_GeoDictionaryBuilder, 20_ThGeoService, 21_AliasService (4 Group 1 modules). Has createPlace() without LockService guard.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Extract geo-enrichment delegation to dedicated service. Add LockService to all write operations. Consider Strategy pattern for search methods.

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,000 lines combining CRUD + search + merge + caching logic
architecture_tech_debt.description: PersonService handles person creation, 5-strategy candidate search, phonetic matching, alias lookup, record merging, statistics updates, and RAM cache management. Missing LockService on createPerson().
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Public functions: resolvePerson(), createPerson(), findPersonCandidates(), mergePersonRecords(), updatePersonStats(), loadAllPersons_(), countPersons()
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Split into: PersonCrud (~300 lines), PersonSearch (~350 lines), PersonMerge (~200 lines), PersonCache (~150 lines)

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,227 lines orchestrating API fetch + transform + enrich + summarize + UI
architecture_tech_debt.description: ServiceSCG handles SCG API authentication, data fetching, coordinate application, owner/shipment summary building, sheet clearing, and UI dialogs. Contains 22+ functions including destructive operations like clearAllSCGSheets_UI().
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Functions: fetchDataFromSCGJWD(), applyMasterCoordinatesToDailyJob(), setSCGCookie_UI(), buildOwnerSummary(), buildShipmentSummary(), clearAllSCGSheets_UI(), safeResetTransactional_UI()
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Split into: SCGApiClient (~300 lines), SCGTransformer (~250 lines), SCGEnricher (~200 lines), SCGSummarizer (~200 lines), SCGUIController (~150 lines)
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Pipeline Stage Extraction / UI Controller Separation

architecture_tech_debt.title: God File: 1,056 lines mixing queue management + decision execution + fact writing
architecture_tech_debt.description: ReviewService handles Q_REVIEW enqueue/dequeue, decision application (MERGE/CREATE_NEW/ESCALATE/IGNORE), FACT_DELIVERY direct writing, and UI highlighting. applyAllPendingDecisions() alone is 200+ lines.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Functions: enqueueReview(), applyReviewDecision(), applyAllPendingDecisions(), getReviewStats(), highlightHighPriorityReviews(), clearDoneReviews_UI() plus 15+ private helpers
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Extract decision execution to ReviewExecutor.gs. Keep only queue management in ReviewService. Route all FACT writes through TransactionService.

architecture_tech_debt.title: Missing LockService guard on Person creation - race condition risk
architecture_tech_debt.description: createPerson() does NOT use LockService.tryLock() before write operations. Concurrent pipeline executions could create duplicate person records or cause data corruption during read-modify-write cycles.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Add LockService.getScriptLock().tryLock(5000) with try-finally release pattern to ALL create* functions across 06/07/08/09 services.

architecture_tech_debt.title: Missing LockService guard on Place creation
architecture_tech_debt.description: createPlace() lacks explicit lock guard. Combined with ATD-005 (tight coupling), this creates a compounded risk where concurrent calls from multiple pipeline stages could corrupt M_PLACE data.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: Same pattern as ATD-009 - no LockService.acquire before sheet write operations
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Standardize LockService wrapper: acquireScriptLockOrWarn_() already exists in 14_Utils.gs - use it consistently.

architecture_tech_debt.title: Missing LockService guard on GeoPoint creation
architecture_tech_debt.description: createGeoPoint() has NO LockService guard despite being a critical write operation. Geo points are created during both pipeline processing and manual admin operations, increasing collision risk.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Apply standard LockService pattern. Geo point creation should be serialized to prevent duplicate coordinates for same location.

architecture_tech_debt.title: appendRow() usage instead of batch setValues()
architecture_tech_debt.description: 10_MatchEngine uses sheet.appendRow(row) for single-row inserts. While frequency may be low for audit trail entries, this sets inconsistent precedent when all other modules use batch setValues().
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Replace with batch collection + single setValues() call. Use same pattern as 10d_MatchTestHarness which correctly batches results.

architecture_tech_debt.title: WebApp Action layer contains direct sheet read/write operations
architecture_tech_debt.description: submitReviewDecision() in WebAppActions directly reads/writes Q_REVIEW and FACT_DELIVERY sheets instead of delegating to 12_ReviewService. This blurs the boundary between presentation layer and service layer.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Extract review submission logic to 12_ReviewService.submitReviewFromWebApp_(). WebAppActions should be thin validation + auth wrapper only.

architecture_tech_debt.law_violated: Law 9: No Global State / Law 20: Cache Invalidation Chain
architecture_tech_debt.title: Module-level mutable cache not reset between executions
architecture_tech_debt.description: _SOURCE_ROWS_RAM_CACHE is declared at module level as mutable state. While this is documented as intentional for performance, it risks stale data if GAS runtime reuses execution context unexpectedly.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Add cache invalidation check based on timestamp or generation counter. Document lifecycle clearly in header comments.

architecture_tech_debt.law_violated: Law 3: Layered Architecture / Separation of Concerns
architecture_tech_debt.title: View layer contains aggregation/computation business logic
architecture_tech_debt.description: 22b_WebAppViews contains computeFactStats_(), computeReviewStats_(), computeDeliveryTrend7Days_() and other aggregation functions. These represent business logic that belongs in ReportService or a dedicated Analytics service.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Move computation functions to 13_ReportService.gs. Views should call pre-computed data providers only.
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Push-down Computation Pattern / Smart UI Pattern

architecture_tech_debt.law_violated: Law 8: Namespace Pattern / Circular Dependency Risk
architecture_tech_debt.title: MatchEngine has excessive fan-out (14+ dependencies)
architecture_tech_debt.description: 10_MatchEngine depends on 14+ other modules: 04, 05, 06, 07, 08, 09, 10b, 10d, 10e, 10f, 10g, 11, 12, 14, 21, 26. This creates tight coupling where changes in any dependency can affect MatchEngine behavior.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Consider introducing MatchContext object that encapsulates dependencies. Use Dependency Injection for strategy services (normalizer, resolvers). Reduce direct function calls across module boundaries.
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Context Object Pattern / Dependency Injection Container

architecture_tech_debt.title: Trinity resolution order may not be enforced consistently
architecture_tech_debt.description: The Trinity Framework requires Person → Place → Geo → Destination resolution order. While 10e implements this, there are multiple code paths (via 12b_ReviewReprocessor) that may call resolvers out of order or skip steps.
architecture_tech_debt.evidence: reprocResolveOrCreatePersonForReview_(), reprocResolveOrCreatePlaceForReview_(), reprocCreateDestinationForReview_() are called separately rather than through unified Trinity orchestrator
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Create enforceTrinityOrder_() validator that ensures Person_ID and PLACE_ID exist before DESTINATION creation. Call this as pre-condition in all persist paths.

architecture_tech_debt.law_violated: Hybrid Alias Architecture - Single Writer Exception
architecture_tech_debt.title: M_ALIAS has dual writers (by design but needs documentation)
architecture_tech_debt.description: Both 21_AliasService.createGlobalAlias() (manual/HUMAN source) and 10f_MatchAliasEnrichment.autoEnrichAliasesFromFactBatch_() (AUTO source) write to M_ALIAS. This is an intentional exception to Single Writer rule but must be documented as approved architectural decision.
architecture_tech_debt.fix_suggestion: Add ARCHITECTURAL_DECISION.md entry documenting this exception. Ensure source field is always populated to distinguish writer origin.
architecture_tech_debt.refactoring_pattern: Documented Exception Pattern / Audit Trail Correlation

sprint_0_quick_wins.change: Replace appendRow() with batch setValues() for pipeline log writes
sprint_0_quick_wins.test_plan: Run 10d_MatchTestHarness dry run - verify PIPELINE_RUN_LOG entries match expected format
Unit test: call createPerson twice concurrently - second should wait or fail gracefully

sprint_0_quick_wins.change: Add LockService guard to createPlace_() (same pattern as RF-002)
sprint_0_quick_wins.test_plan: Same as RF-002 - concurrent place creation test

sprint_0_quick_wins.change: Add LockService guard to createGeoPoint_() (same pattern as RF-002)

sprint_0_quick_wins.test_plan: Integration test: apply review decision → verify FACT_DELIVERY written via TransactionService only

sprint_3_polish.change: Create ARCHITECTURAL_DECISIONS.md documenting M_ALIAS dual-writer exception
sprint_3_polish.before: // No documentation of Single Writer exceptions
sprint_3_polish.after:
# Architectural Decisions Log

## AD-001: M_ALIAS Dual-Writer Pattern (Approved V6.0)
**Date:** 2026-07-16
**Status:** Approved
**Context:** Hybrid Alias Architecture requires two writers:
- **21_AliasService.createGlobalAlias()** for MANUAL/HUMAN sources (admin UI, migration)
- **10f_MatchAliasEnrichment.autoEnrichAliasesFromFactBatch_()** for AUTO sources (pipeline)
**Mitigation:** `source` field distinguishes origin ('MANUAL' vs 'FACT_DELIVERY')
**Risk:** Low - writers operate on different entity subsets
**Review Date:** Next architecture audit (V7.0)

sprint_3_polish.test_plan: Test: incomplete data → verify graceful REVIEW escalation instead of partial write

R-01_extract_function.when_to_use: Function exceeds 100 lines or handles multiple concerns (SRP violation)

R-02_replace_magic_number.when_to_use: Hardcoded numeric constants (thresholds, timeouts, sizes) found in function body

R-03_single_writer.when_to_use: Multiple functions need to write to same sheet (race condition risk)

R-04_dependency_inversion.when_to_use: Module has high fan-out (many direct dependencies on concrete implementations)

R-05_lock_service_guard.when_to_use: Any function that writes to a Google Sheet (concurrency safety)

R-06_batch_collector.when_to_use: Loop that would otherwise call setValue/appendRow per iteration

R-07_gateway_wrapper.when_to_use: Cross-domain-layer call (e.g., Group 2 calling Group 1 master write)

R-08_thin_controller.when_to_use: Presentation layer (WebApp) needs to trigger business logic

preconditions: Main branch tagged as v6.0.062-pre-refactor before starting
Each sprint executed on feature branch (feature/arch-refactor-sprint-N)

steps.action: If sprint N causes test failures:
If critical bug found in production:
commands: Re-apply later sprints after hotfix verified

steps.action: If need to undo single refactoring within sprint:
commands: Use git revert SHA (not reset) to preserve history
Run full test suite after revert
Document reason for revert in CHANGELOG

not_yet_checked: Live concurrent test (ตรวจสอบ race condition จริงบน Google Apps Script environment)
Performance benchmark (ตรวจสอบว่า refactor ไม่ทำให้ช้าลง - ต้องรันใน Apps Script จริง)
GAS execution time จริง (ต้องรัน clasp push และ deploy จริงเพื่อวัดเวลา)
Cross-sheet data integrity (ต้องตรวจสอบกับ Google Sheet จริงที่มีข้อมูล production)
Memory usage under load (CacheService 100KB limit stress test)
UrlFetch quota consumption under typical daily workload (20K/day limit)

recommendation_details.condition: GO for production IF Sprint 0 (Quick Wins) completed first
recommendation_details.rationale: P0 issues #1-#2 (Single Writer violations by Group 2) and #3-#5 (Missing LockService on all CRUD writes) MUST be resolved before production deployment. These represent data corruption risk under concurrent load. The God Files (P1) are maintainability concerns that should be addressed in Sprint 1-2 but do not block initial deployment if code review process is strong.
blocking_issues: AUD2-ATD-009/010/011: Missing LockService on all Group 1 CRUD writes
non_blocking_but_important: AUD2-ATD-003/004/005/006/007/008: God Files (>1000 lines) - technical debt accumulation risk
AUD2-ATD-016: Excessive MatchEngine fan-out (14+ deps) - change amplification risk
positive_patterns_found: Single Writer for M_ALIAS properly implemented (10f + 21 with source tracking)
Pipeline Manager properly scoped with circuit breaker (24_PipelineManager)
Frontend zero business logic leakage (clean HTML/JS separation)
RBAC 3-role system complete with permission matrix (27_RbacService)
Batch operations correctly used in most modules (setValues pattern)
IDX constants usage consistent (no hardcoded indices found)
Audit Trail implementation append-only with retention pruning (26_AuditTrailService)
